title: cymbopogon marginatus
intro: Cymbopogon marginatus, also known as the lemon-scented grass or East Indian lemon grass, is a species of grass native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The essential oil extracted from the plant has been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing stress and anxiety, as well as improving cardiovascular health. Cymbopogon marginatus can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, oils, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
